methodology: 2-iteration adversarial research process across 11 GICS sectors. V1 average score 67, V2 average score 82.6 (+15.6 improvement). Each sector underwent independent expert analysis followed by adversarial judge review. V2 addressed all systemic flaws: added SELL recommendations to every sector, included position sizing with stop-losses, scenario analysis with probability weighting, commodity/rate sensitivity tables, and corrected all scoring math.

portfolio_summary.googl_resolution: GOOGL counted once under Communication Services (primary coverage). Technology report references GOOGL for Cloud/AI but defers rating to Comms.
portfolio_summary.portfolio_construction_note: This portfolio is designed for a diversified equity allocation. Sector weights reflect conviction-adjusted exposure, not benchmark tracking. Maximum single-name position: 5%. Maximum sector allocation: 20%.

top_picks.name: Broadcom Inc.

bear_case.rationale: AI ASIC demand disappoints vs $73B backlog, VMware churn emerges, debt servicing pressure. PE compresses to 18x.

base_case.rationale: AI semiconductor revenue continues doubling, VMware integration on track, debt reduction accelerates. Forward PE 25x.

bull_case.rationale: AI backlog converts faster, VMware margins above 80%, third hyperscaler adopts XPU. Forward PE 28x.

top_picks.thesis_summary: Best PEG ratio (0.68) in semiconductors with dual AI semiconductor + VMware engine, $73B AI backlog, 42% FCF margin. Most complete risk-reward package in the entire coverage universe.
top_picks.key_catalyst: Q2 FY2026 earnings in June with $22B revenue guide validation and expanding AI backlog
top_picks.biggest_risk: VMware integration execution and debt servicing; NVIDIA networking competition

top_picks.name: Alphabet Inc.

bear_case.rationale: DOJ forces structural separation, search revenue declines, capex destroys FCF. PE compresses to 18x.

base_case.rationale: Cloud sustains 35%+ growth, search resilient, capex generates adequate returns. PE expands to 28x.

bull_case.rationale: Cloud hits 50%+ growth, Waymo monetization begins, DOJ remedy is mild. PE expands to 32x.

top_picks.thesis_summary: Best risk-reward in mega-cap tech at 24.5x PE with 18% revenue growth, fastest-growing cloud (48%), antitrust risk largely priced in. Comms report owns primary coverage.
top_picks.key_catalyst: Q1 2026 earnings showing continued cloud acceleration above 40% and Gemini adoption metrics
top_picks.biggest_risk: DOJ antitrust remedies; massive capex destroying near-term FCF

top_picks.name: Meta Platforms Inc.

bear_case.rationale: Ad recession hits both GOOGL and META simultaneously, Reality Labs losses escalate. PE compresses to 16x ex-RL.

base_case.rationale: AI-driven CPM uplift sustains, FoA revenue grows 16%. Multiple re-rates toward 22x.

bull_case.rationale: Meta AI monetization, Ray-Ban glasses mainstream. PE 25x on core business.

top_picks.thesis_summary: Core Family of Apps trades at just 18.1x PE ex-Reality Labs, cheaper than S&P 500. AI-driven 10% CPM uplift on $200B+ ad base generates $8-12B incremental revenue. FTC dismissed.
top_picks.key_catalyst: Meta AI assistant monetization; AI-driven CPM improvements accelerating
top_picks.biggest_risk: Reality Labs burning $19-20B annually with no profitability path; CapEx overshoot

top_picks.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.

bear_case.rationale: Taiwan geopolitical escalation or semiconductor cycle downturn. PE compresses to 15x.

base_case.rationale: 30% revenue growth with margin expansion, 2nm ramp successful. PE 25x.

bull_case.rationale: Growth exceeds 35%, Intel foundry struggles boost TSM. PE 30x on monopoly premium.

top_picks.thesis_summary: Deepest moat in semiconductors -- irreplaceable manufacturing monopoly at the leading edge. Position capped at 4% due to Taiwan geopolitical binary risk despite 9.0 score.
top_picks.key_catalyst: Q1 2026 earnings April 17 showing 38% YoY revenue growth with 2nm ramp
top_picks.biggest_risk: Taiwan Strait geopolitical escalation -- binary risk requiring position size cap

top_picks.name: Eli Lilly and Company

bear_case.rationale: GLP-1 growth slows to 15%, IRA negotiates Mounjaro, competitive erosion from MariTide.

base_case.rationale: 20% revenue CAGR, moderate 5% net price decline starting 2028.

bull_case.rationale: Pricing power maintained, oral orforglipron expands TAM.

top_picks.thesis_summary: GLP-1 market leader with 60% US share. Revenue guidance of $80-83B implies 25% growth, highest in large-cap pharma. Medicare Part D copay cap is a volume catalyst.
top_picks.key_catalyst: Orforglipron oral obesity pill Phase 3 data; Medicare Part D Zepbound volume ramp
top_picks.biggest_risk: GLP-1 pricing pressure from IRA negotiation and competitive crowding

top_picks.name: AbbVie Inc.

bear_case.rationale: IRA negotiates Rinvoq/Skyrizi at 40% discount. Humira erosion accelerates.

base_case.rationale: Skyrizi/Rinvoq combined exceed $31.6B guidance. Patent protection holds through 2033-2037.

bull_case.rationale: IBD franchise expansion, dividend growth, neuroscience recovery.

top_picks.thesis_summary: Franchise transition executing: Skyrizi/Rinvoq at $31.6B combined guidance exceeds peak Humira. At 14x 2026 EPS with 3.5% yield and 54-year dividend streak, best value in large-cap pharma.
top_picks.key_catalyst: Skyrizi/Rinvoq sales exceeding guidance; patent protection confirmation
top_picks.biggest_risk: IRA pricing risk on Rinvoq/Skyrizi in 2027-2029 cycles

top_picks.name: Cheniere Energy

bear_case.rationale: LNG spot collapse AND credit tightening. EPS compresses to $10-14 at normalized JKM.

base_case.rationale: Contracted cash flows provide floor. CCL Stage 3 completes. Secular LNG demand growth.

bull_case.rationale: Sustained elevated LNG premiums from Hormuz. Sabine Pass expansion FID.

top_picks.thesis_summary: Highest-conviction energy pick. 90-95% contracted volumes provide cash flow visibility regardless of commodity price. Secular LNG demand survives any oil scenario.
top_picks.key_catalyst: CCL Stage 3 completion; potential Sabine Pass expansion FID
top_picks.biggest_risk: Simultaneous LNG spot collapse AND credit market tightening; LNG oversupply post-2028

top_picks.name: CRH plc

bear_case.rationale: Infrastructure spending flat, residential -10%. EBITDA compresses to $7.2B.

base_case.rationale: IIJA peak disbursement, residential recovery. EBITDA $8.3B per guidance.

bull_case.rationale: Full infrastructure + data center boom. EBITDA $9.0B with M&A synergies.

top_picks.thesis_summary: Best risk-adjusted return in materials. Visible IIJA earnings runway, proven M&A value creation (ROIC 8.4% to 11.9%), 18.5x PE with infrastructure fiscal tailwind.
top_picks.key_catalyst: IIJA disbursement acceleration; continued value-creating M&A
top_picks.biggest_risk: Infrastructure spending delays; acquisition integration risk

top_picks.name: Philip Morris International

bear_case.rationale: FDA denies IQOS Iluma; state-level ZYN taxes. PE compresses to 18x.

base_case.rationale: 11-13% EPS growth delivers, IQOS FDA approval. PE stable at 22x.

bull_case.rationale: IQOS US re-entry, ZYN capacity expansion. PE expands to 24x on growth re-rate.

top_picks.thesis_summary: Only consumer staples company delivering double-digit EPS growth through genuine structural transformation. IQOS and ZYN are category-defining with dominant share. 3.56% yield at 22x PE.
top_picks.key_catalyst: FDA IQOS Iluma approval (55% probability, H1-H2 2026); ZYN capacity expansion
top_picks.biggest_risk: FDA denial or material delay on IQOS; state-level nicotine pouch taxes

top_picks.name: NVIDIA Corporation

bear_case.rationale: Hyperscaler capex rationalization, AMD MI450 takes share, semiconductor cycle peaks. PE 15x on $280B rev.

base_case.rationale: FY2027 revenue $310-320B (45% growth), competitive erosion recognized but 70%+ share held. PE 20x.

bull_case.rationale: Revenue exceeds $350B, Blackwell Ultra new cycle, sovereign AI demand. PE 25x.

top_picks.thesis_summary: Essential AI infrastructure provider but moat narrowing from monopoly to oligopoly leader. At 21x forward with 50%+ growth, strong risk-adjusted exposure. No longer max-conviction.
top_picks.key_catalyst: Blackwell Ultra GPU launch and $78B Q1 FY2027 revenue validation in May 2026
top_picks.biggest_risk: Customer concentration (61% top 4); competitive displacement from AMD/Google/Broadcom

top_picks.name: Vertex Pharmaceuticals

bear_case.rationale: JOURNAVX commercial adoption slower than expected; CF revenue plateau.

base_case.rationale: JOURNAVX prescriptions triple; CASGEVY ramp; kidney pipeline advances.

bull_case.rationale: JOURNAVX blockbuster, multiple pipeline catalysts succeed.

top_picks.thesis_summary: Building beyond CF dominance (95% share) with stacked 2026-2028 catalyst calendar. JOURNAVX, CASGEVY, and povetacicept provide $1B+ launch opportunities.
top_picks.key_catalyst: JOURNAVX prescription tripling in 2026; povetacicept for kidney disease
top_picks.biggest_risk: CF still vast majority of revenue; diversification directional not achieved

top_picks.name: Boston Scientific Corporation

bear_case.rationale: Penumbra integration issues; EP competition intensifies; tariff headwinds.

base_case.rationale: 10-11% organic growth delivers; FARAPULSE dominance; structural heart expansion.

bull_case.rationale: FARAPULSE monopolizes EP; neuromodulation accelerates; M&A synergies.

top_picks.thesis_summary: Strongest organic growth in large-cap medtech at 10-11% guidance. FARAPULSE, structural heart, and neuromodulation all growing double digits.
top_picks.key_catalyst: FARAPULSE pulsed field ablation market expansion; Penumbra integration
top_picks.biggest_risk: Penumbra acquisition at $14.5B carries integration and ROIC risk

top_picks.name: Lockheed Martin Corporation

bear_case.rationale: Bear defense budget at $1.05T. LMT EPS drops to $26.62.

base_case.rationale: $1.25T budget, EPS $32.37. F-35 record deliveries, missile ramps.

bull_case.rationale: Full $1.5T budget passes. LMT EPS $34.79.

top_picks.thesis_summary: Top defense pick: best valuation (29x PE lowest among industrial picks), firmest backlog quality (80% firm orders of $194B), most direct defense budget exposure.
top_picks.key_catalyst: FY2027 defense budget passage; missile production ramp acceleration
top_picks.biggest_risk: Defense budget sequestration; F-35 sustainment cost overruns

top_picks.name: Amazon.com Inc.

bear_case.rationale: Stagflation: tariff-driven inflation, consumer pullback, AWS slowdown.

base_case.rationale: Consumer spending normalizes, 3P growth accelerates, advertising scales.

bull_case.rationale: Consumer confidence rebound, 3P and advertising escape velocity. Multiple 35x.

top_picks.thesis_summary: World's largest e-commerce platform at $440B+ retail revenue. PE of 28.9x is 39% below 3-year average. Retail flywheel + 3P shift + $85B advertising run rate.
top_picks.key_catalyst: Q1 2026 earnings showing 3P mix improvement and advertising acceleration
top_picks.biggest_risk: Consumer credit stress reducing volumes; $200B capex cycle compressing FCF

top_picks.name: Constellation Energy

bear_case.rationale: Calpine integration issues, Crane restart delay, rates at 5.5%. PE compresses to 20x.

base_case.rationale: Calpine synergy realization, Crane milestone, additional hyperscaler PPAs. PE 26x.

bull_case.rationale: Nuclear renaissance accelerates, power prices spike on AI demand. PE 30x.

top_picks.thesis_summary: Premier US nuclear fleet with contracted hyperscaler PPAs (Microsoft, Meta). Calpine acquisition creates 60 GW portfolio at 7.9x EV/EBITDA. No regulatory lag risk.
top_picks.key_catalyst: Calpine synergy realization in 2H 2026; Crane restart milestone 2027
top_picks.biggest_risk: Calpine integration execution; Crane restart delay; nuclear incident risk

top_picks.name: The TJX Companies Inc.

bear_case.rationale: Severe recession reduces even discount traffic temporarily.

base_case.rationale: Trade-down moderates but positive. Base case.

bull_case.rationale: Trade-down intensifies in recession. Counter-cyclical gains.

top_picks.thesis_summary: Premier tariff beneficiary with 15-year track record of outperforming during trade disruptions. Q4 comps +5% beating estimates by 130bps.
top_picks.key_catalyst: Tariff escalation driving more branded inventory into off-price channel
top_picks.biggest_risk: 31x forward PE rich for single-digit EPS grower if trade-down fades

top_picks.name: Booking Holdings Inc.

bear_case.rationale: Stagflation raises airfare costs, European weakness deepens.

base_case.rationale: Travel demand robust, connected trip drives ARPU. Multiple normalizes.

bull_case.rationale: Pent-up travel demand, business travel fully recovers. Multiple 25x+.

top_picks.thesis_summary: Best risk-reward in consumer discretionary travel. 15.6x forward PE is genuinely cheap (19% below 10-year median). $9.1B FCF, 36.9% EBITDA margins.
top_picks.key_catalyst: Summer travel season; AI trip planning; buybacks at depressed valuation
top_picks.biggest_risk: Geopolitical tensions dampening international travel; AI disintermediating OTAs

top_picks.name: MercadoLibre Inc.

bear_case.rationale: Brazilian recession pushes NPLs to 8-10%; LatAm FX depreciation.

base_case.rationale: Secular LatAm e-commerce penetration continues. Credit book stabilizes.

bull_case.rationale: LatAm digital transformation accelerates; fintech achieves scale economics.

top_picks.thesis_summary: LatAm Amazon/PayPal hybrid with secular growth. E-commerce penetration a decade behind US. Credit book NPL improving (6.8% to 4.4% Q3-Q4). 38x forward PE for 30%+ growth.
top_picks.key_catalyst: Continued LatAm e-commerce penetration; credit book stabilization
top_picks.biggest_risk: Brazilian recession spiking NPLs to 8-10%; currency depreciation

top_picks.name: Eaton Corporation

bear_case.rationale: AI capex rationalization, tariff headwinds on copper/aluminum. PE 22x.

base_case.rationale: Data center orders sustain, Electrical Americas 30% margins. EPS $14.80 at 27x.

bull_case.rationale: Hyperscaler capex expands, mobility spin-off unlocks value. PE 30x.

top_picks.thesis_summary: Purest play on electrification. Data center orders up 200% on substantial base. 11-year construction backlog. Electrical Americas targeting 30% margins.
top_picks.key_catalyst: Hyperscaler CapEx reaffirmation for 2026; utility grid modernization
top_picks.biggest_risk: AI capex rationalization (15-20% probability); tariff headwinds on copper/aluminum

top_picks.name: Netflix Inc.

bear_case.rationale: Content cost escalation, ad tier CPM compression, macro sensitivity.

base_case.rationale: Ad tier doubles to $3B, operating margin expands to 31.5%. Live sports engagement.

bull_case.rationale: Ad tier exceeds $4B, international APAC/LATAM expansion accelerates.

top_picks.thesis_summary: Streaming winner with ad tier optionality. 190M ad-tier MAU, $3B 2026 target is floor not ceiling. Content spend at 39% of revenue declining as ratio.
top_picks.key_catalyst: Ad tier revenue doubling; live sports engagement; operating margin expansion to 31.5%
top_picks.biggest_risk: Content cost inflation; ad cannibalization of premium plans

top_picks.name: T-Mobile US Inc.

bear_case.rationale: Competitive pricing pressure; subscriber growth misses; acquisition costs.

base_case.rationale: Fixed wireless disrupts cable; US Cellular integration; EPS 9.3% growth.

bull_case.rationale: Cable disintermediation accelerates; 5G enterprise monetization.

top_picks.thesis_summary: Best-positioned US telecom and critical portfolio diversifier (0.15 correlation with ad-dependent names). Fixed wireless disrupting cable. 37% upside to consensus.
top_picks.key_catalyst: Fixed wireless broadband reaching critical mass; US Cellular integration
top_picks.biggest_risk: AT&T/Verizon catching up on 5G; regulatory scrutiny of acquisitions

top_picks.name: Linde plc

bear_case.rationale: Mild recession, -5% industrial volumes. EPS $15.50.

base_case.rationale: 2-3% volume growth, EPS $17.65 midpoint guidance. Hydrogen secular tailwind.

bull_case.rationale: Semi boom + hydrogen buildout. Full capacity. EPS $19.50.

top_picks.thesis_summary: Highest quality through-cycle compounder in materials. Duopoly market structure, cost-plus contracts, 33.5x PE at 10-year median. Hydrogen/semiconductor secular growth.
top_picks.key_catalyst: Hydrogen economy buildout; semiconductor fab construction demand
top_picks.biggest_risk: PE expansion to 35x+ would warrant trimming; industrial recession

top_picks.name: PepsiCo Inc.

bear_case.rationale: GLP-1 acceleration, continued Frito-Lay share losses, FX headwinds.

base_case.rationale: Multiple expansion from trough + 4% yield + Lay's relaunch inflection.

bull_case.rationale: Activist interest, permissible snacking pivot succeeds, away-from-home expansion.

top_picks.thesis_summary: Deepest value in mega-cap staples at 20.4x PE (20% below 5yr avg). 4% Dividend King yield. Permissible snacking pivot addresses GLP-1 headwind.
top_picks.key_catalyst: Lay's/Tostitos natural ingredient relaunch; PE mean reversion
top_picks.biggest_risk: GLP-1 adoption exceeding 30% household penetration; Frito-Lay share losses

top_picks.name: Vistra Corp

bear_case.rationale: Leverage at 3.36x amplifies any earnings miss; power price volatility.

base_case.rationale: 2026 EBITDA $6.8-7.6B delivers. Meta PPA provides stability. FCF funds buyback.

bull_case.rationale: Power prices spike on AI demand; deleveraging accelerates.

top_picks.thesis_summary: Largest US unregulated power producer with 6,400 MW nuclear capacity backed by 20-year Meta PPA. 2026 EBITDA guidance up 65% YoY. Forward PE 18.7x.
top_picks.key_catalyst: 2026 EBITDA delivery proving guidance credibility; deleveraging progress below 3x D/E
top_picks.biggest_risk: Leverage at 3.36x D/E in distress zone; nuclear operational risk amplified by leverage

top_picks.name: The Coca-Cola Company

bear_case.rationale: IRS tax ruling at full $18B liability; GLP-1 exceeds projections.

base_case.rationale: 7-8% EPS growth + 2.8% yield. EM drives double-digit organic.

bull_case.rationale: IRS resolution favorable; zero-sugar innovation; EM acceleration.

top_picks.thesis_summary: Widest moat in consumer staples. 200+ country distribution, 63 consecutive dividend increases. At 25.3x (in line with 5yr avg), fair price for highest quality franchise.
top_picks.key_catalyst: EM volume acceleration (India, Africa); IRS tax dispute resolution
top_picks.biggest_risk: IRS tax ruling at full liability; GLP-1 adoption exceeding projections

top_picks.name: VICI Properties Inc.

bear_case.rationale: Rising rates compress long-duration income stream. 16% price decline at +100bps.

base_case.rationale: 16% NAV discount narrows; CPI escalators provide income growth. 6.7% yield.

bull_case.rationale: Rate cuts drive multiple re-rating. 10% total return reprices to 8%.

top_picks.thesis_summary: Income anchor: 6.7% yield + 16% NAV discount + CPI escalators + 100% occupancy + 39.6-year average lease term. Total return justified vs Treasuries by wide margin.
top_picks.key_catalyst: Rate environment stabilization; experiential real estate expansion
top_picks.biggest_risk: Rising rates compress long-duration income stream sharply

top_picks.name: American Tower Corporation

bear_case.rationale: Rates rise to 5.5%; international FX headwinds; Sprint churn lingers.

base_case.rationale: Sprint churn ends; CoreSite AI growth; multiple re-rates toward historical 21.9x.

bull_case.rationale: Rate cuts drive re-rating; CoreSite becomes major growth driver.

top_picks.thesis_summary: Deepest NAV discount (19%) among REIT buys. CoreSite data centers worth ~$30/share. Sprint churn ending removes key overhang. Best risk/reward in REIT universe.
top_picks.key_catalyst: Sprint churn completion; CoreSite AI-driven growth
top_picks.biggest_risk: International tower exposure to FX volatility; rate sensitivity at 5x leverage

top_picks.name: EOG Resources

bear_case.rationale: Oil normalizes to $60-70 WTI. EPS compresses to $5.52-$7.92. PE 17x.

base_case.rationale: Best-in-class operator, special dividends at $90+ oil, multi-basin diversification.

bull_case.rationale: Sustained high oil, well productivity improvements, emerging basin success.

top_picks.thesis_summary: Defensive E&P play -- best-in-class operator with lowest cost structure, most conservative management. If you must own upstream, EOG loses least in downturns.
top_picks.key_catalyst: Special dividends at $90+ oil; technology-driven well productivity improvements
top_picks.biggest_risk: Commodity price decline to $60-70 WTI compresses earnings 50%

sell_list.name: Chevron
sell_list.rationale: Highest breakeven among majors at $50/bbl. At $70 WTI forward curve, PE balloons to 32.5x. Up 33% YTD pricing best-case. Forward curve disagrees at $75 Dec 2026.

sell_list.name: SLB (Schlumberger)
sell_list.rationale: OFS earnings lag oil by 6-12 months. Q1 revenue warning is just the beginning. At $70 WTI, E&P capex cut 15-25%.

sell_list.name: Intel Corporation
sell_list.rationale: Value trap masquerading as turnaround. Negative FCF of -$4.5B. Foundry lost $2.51B operationally. Morningstar rates 33% overvalued.

sell_list.name: Dell Technologies
sell_list.rationale: Low-margin AI server assembler with minimal moat. 3-5% operating margins. Hyperscaler direct procurement shrinks Dell's role.

sell_list.name: Merck & Co.
sell_list.rationale: Keytruda $32.7B peak at risk from 2028 patent expiry -- largest single-drug cliff in history. Januvia/Lynparza compound gap.

sell_list.name: Pfizer Inc.
sell_list.rationale: $21B revenue gap from Prevnar 2026, Ibrance/Xtandi 2027 patent losses. Seagen/Metsera too early. Value trap until pipeline executes.

sell_list.name: BlackRock Inc.
sell_list.rationale: TTM P/E 26.5x is 32% above 10-year median. GIP integration unproven. Fee compression structural. AUM-dependent in recession.

sell_list.name: Nike Inc.
sell_list.rationale: Import-dependent brand facing 50% tariffs. Consumer confidence at recessionary levels. Market share losses to On, Hoka, New Balance.

sell_list.name: Walmart Inc.
sell_list.rationale: 46x PE for a retailer growing 5% revenue is historically unprecedented. Ad revenue optionality is real but prices in 5+ years of perfect execution.

sell_list.name: Colgate-Palmolive
sell_list.rationale: 33.4x PE with only 1.4% revenue growth. Premium unjustifiable vs sector. Household products private label share rising.

sell_list.name: Hershey Company
sell_list.rationale: Cocoa crisis + GLP-1 double whammy. 85% revenue in exposed confectionery. 52x PE on depressed earnings with no near-term recovery.

sell_list.name: NextEra Energy
sell_list.rationale: Most expensive large-cap utility at 25.3x with least near-term data center leverage. Behind-the-meter generation competes with renewable PPA model.

sell_list.name: SBA Communications
sell_list.rationale: 7x debt/EBITDA highest in REIT universe. Sprint churn $55-56M headwind. At +100bps rates: -22% price decline, -5% AFFO.

sell_list.name: Crown Castle
sell_list.rationale: 6x leverage + negative FFO growth is worst combination in REIT universe. DISH $220M churn. 6.3% yield is a trap.

watchlist.name: Intuitive Surgical
watchlist.rationale: Exceptional moat but 57x trailing PE. Wait for $375 (47x PE, 10% correction) for better risk-reward.

watchlist.name: Diamondback Energy
watchlist.rationale: Value trap at $110 oil. At $70 WTI, PE expands to 18.6x. Only add below $180 (implies ~$70 WTI pricing).

watchlist.name: ExxonMobil
watchlist.rationale: Best assets but stock price already reflects it. HOLD; add only on 25%+ pullback.

watchlist.name: ConocoPhillips
watchlist.rationale: Organic decline of 2.6% ex-Marathon + peak-cycle earnings. HOLD; do not add.

watchlist.name: Costco
watchlist.rationale: Exceptional business at excessive 53x PE. Wait for pullback to 40x.

watchlist.name: GE Vernova
watchlist.rationale: EBITDA margin bridge to 20% requires flawless execution. At 45x trailing, priced for perfection. HOLD; add below $280.

watchlist.name: Quanta Services
watchlist.rationale: 83x trailing PE at 5.8% margins. No industrial services company sustained 40x+ for 2+ years. HOLD.

watchlist.name: Albemarle
watchlist.rationale: SELL now; re-enter below $95 or when lithium sustains $22+/kg.

watchlist.name: Newmont
watchlist.rationale: Portfolio hedge, not core long. Gold above $4,500 implies macro stress that hurts equity portfolios. Tactical only.

watchlist.name: Walt Disney Company
watchlist.rationale: BUY-rated but close to trigger. ESPN standalone is binary catalyst. Add aggressively below $90.

maximum_sector_allocations.technology: 16% (AVGO 5%, TSM 4%, NVDA 3.5%, AMD not in top 28 but monitor)
maximum_sector_allocations.industrials: 9.5% (LMT 3.5%, ETN 3%, TDG not in top 28 but at 3% in sector alloc, GE at 4%)
maximum_sector_allocations.financials: 0% in top picks but see sector report for JPM, V, GS positions

position_sizing_guidelines.rebalance_triggers: Any position exceeding 7% or sector exceeding 22%

portfolio_level_stop_loss.trailing_stop: If portfolio drawdown exceeds 15% from peak, reduce all positions by 25% and move to 35% cash
portfolio_level_stop_loss.sector_rotation_trigger: If any 2 sectors simultaneously trigger stop-losses, shift 10% to defensive (staples, utilities, healthcare)

correlation_warnings: AI infrastructure concentration: AVGO + NVDA + TSM + ETN + CEG + VST all benefit from hyperscaler capex. If AI spending disappoints, correlation spike to 0.8+ across these names.
Digital advertising concentration: GOOGL + META have 0.87 ad revenue correlation. Combined allocation capped at 9.5%. In 20% ad downturn, both decline 25-35%.
Energy commodity correlation: LNG + EOG have >0.80 correlation with WTI. At 5.5% combined allocation, manageable.
Interest rate sensitivity: VICI, AMT, and all utilities are rate-sensitive. In +100bps scenario, combined drawdown of 15-20%. Total rate-sensitive allocation: 9%.
Consumer credit sensitivity: AMZN, MELI, BKNG all have consumer spending exposure. MELI has highest credit risk (LatAm NPLs).
hedging_recommendations: Buy USO put spreads ($70/$55) for Jun/Sep 2026 to hedge energy positions. Cost: 0.3% of portfolio for 40% downside protection.
Maintain 10% cash buffer for tactical opportunities. Current environment favors selectivity over full deployment.
Consider XLY put options to hedge consumer discretionary exposure if consumer confidence index drops below 65.
For AI capex hedging: if any two hyperscalers (MSFT, GOOG, AMZN, META) cut capex guidance by 15%+, reduce tech + industrials by 30%.
Gold allocation (NEM at 2% max) as portfolio insurance, not conviction long.

cross_sector_themes.risk: Hyperscaler capex at 4.4% of GDP approaches dot-com peak ratios. If AI monetization timelines extend, simultaneous correction across all AI-linked positions. Historical analog: dot-com fiber overbuild saw $65B+ investment with years of overcapacity.
cross_sector_themes.mitigation: Diversify across AI value chain (semis, power, software, infrastructure). Avoid 100% AI-dependent names. Monitor hyperscaler capex-to-FCF ratios quarterly.

cross_sector_themes.risk: Hormuz crisis driving $31/bbl geopolitical premium. Forward curve at $75 WTI by Dec 2026. Historical analog: 3 of 4 geopolitical spikes saw energy stocks return -5% to -55% in 12-18 months after peak.
cross_sector_themes.mitigation: All energy positions sized for $74 WTI base case, not $99 spot. Put spread hedges in place. Overweight midstream (LNG) vs E&P.

cross_sector_themes.risk: 23% US household penetration rising. At 30%+ penetration, food/beverage volume declines accelerate beyond current estimates.
cross_sector_themes.mitigation: Overweight GLP-1 beneficiaries (LLY). Underweight pure snack/confectionery. Monitor quarterly GLP-1 prescription data.

cross_sector_themes.risk: 50% duties on steel/aluminum/copper creating Aluminum Squeeze. 86% of consumers anxious about tariff impacts.
cross_sector_themes.mitigation: Overweight counter-cyclical off-price (TJX). Avoid import-dependent brands. Monitor tariff negotiation signals.

cross_sector_themes.risk: $1.5T proposal requires Congressional approval. Base case $1.2-1.3T realized. Fiscal hawks credible constraint.
cross_sector_themes.mitigation: Size defense positions for base case ($1.25T), not bull case ($1.5T). Monitor reconciliation vote progress.

cross_sector_themes.risk: 10-year at 4.38% already exceeds utility dividend yields. Move to 5.5% could trigger 20%+ drawdowns in rate-sensitive names.
cross_sector_themes.mitigation: Total rate-sensitive allocation capped at 9%. Favor unregulated generators (CEG, VST) over regulated utilities. Sell NEE, SBAC, CCI.

cross_sector_themes.description: Names trading at generational discounts to historical multiples with identifiable catalysts for mean reversion
cross_sector_themes.risk: Value traps -- discount is warranted if fundamentals permanently impaired.
cross_sector_themes.mitigation: Require identifiable catalyst within 6 months. Set stop-losses. Monitor quarterly for thesis confirmation.

financials.note: Financials picks (JPM 8.5, V 8.2, GS 8.0, PGR 7.4, C 7.1) are strong but not included in top 28. See sector report for allocation.

cash_reserve.rationale: Elevated cash reflects late-cycle caution. Deploy on pullbacks to watchlist trigger prices.

data_sources.sector_reports: 11 v2 sector reports generated via 2-iteration adversarial research process
data_sources.price_data: Web search April 4, 2026 -- approximate prices from Yahoo Finance, Google Finance, CNBC, MarketBeat, StockAnalysis
data_sources.scenario_analysis: Extracted from individual v2 sector reports where available; synthesized for picks without explicit scenarios
